Annotation

Conclusion and Recommendations based on this report and previous findings known to us
The patient shows elevated creatinine and chloride, suggesting possible renal impairment. Urinalysis reveals significant proteinuria, glucosuria, ketonuria, low specific gravity, and abnormal urine appearance, indicating renal dysfunction and possible tubular damage. HbA1c is low, ruling out chronic hyperglycemia. No evidence of infection. Recommend further nephrological evaluation and assessment for acute or chronic kidney disease.
